question: What is your financial runway? What happens to our data if your company is acquired or goes out of business?
title: Financial Stability, Exit Scenarios, and Data Protection Guarantees
bullets: Disclose financial health (runway, burn rate, revenue, funding/profitability) with concrete metrics
Define contractual protections for acquisition scenarios (notification, continuity of terms, data handling equivalence)
Detail bankruptcy/cessation plans (data return/deletion timelines, escrow arrangements, transition support)
Explain data portability guarantees (formats, access methods, timelines) and customer transition support
suggested_inputs: Summary of financial metrics (runway, funding, revenue mix) under NDA
MSA/DPA clauses covering acquisition, bankruptcy, and data handling
Source code escrow agreement (provider, triggers, scope)
Data export procedures and sample exports (formats, completeness)

good.text: We provide concrete financial metrics (runway in months/years, burn rate, revenue/profitability). Information is shared under NDA to support enterprise due diligence.
Contracts define acquisition scenarios: customer notification, continuity of service and terms, and equivalent or stronger data protection obligations for any successor entity.

In bankruptcy/cessation, we provide documented procedures: source code escrow (with defined release triggers), data export in standard formats, and deletion within a defined timeframe, with transition support (e.g., 60–90 days).
We guarantee full data portability (raw data, configurations, logs) via API/UI in standard formats (JSON/CSV/SQL), with documented timelines and assistance for migration during exit scenarios.

partial.text: We indicate financial stability or funding, but do not provide specific runway, burn rate, or revenue metrics.
Contracts mention continuity and data handling in acquisition/bankruptcy, but lack detailed procedures, timelines, or enforcement clarity.
Source code escrow is available optionally or partially defined, with unclear triggers or scope.
Data export is supported, but completeness (e.g., configurations, logs) or timelines for access during shutdown are not fully guaranteed.

bad.text: Financial information is confidential and cannot be shared even under NDA.
We do not have documented procedures for acquisition or shutdown scenarios.

follow_ups: Provide financial runway (months) and key metrics under NDA (burn rate, revenue, funding).
Share the contract clauses covering acquisition and bankruptcy scenarios (notification, continuity, data handling).
Is source code escrowed? With which provider, and what are the release triggers and scope?
If you shut down tomorrow, how do we retrieve our data (formats, completeness, timelines)?
What transition support do you provide (duration, assistance) during exit scenarios?
What is your customer concentration risk (top 3 customers as % of revenue)?
